title: Transactional Outbox, Relay, Inbox, Sagas, and Compensation · Contract Lens: Inbox Outbox Composition
summary: An HTTP transaction commits database state then publishes, or publishes then rolls back; a relay deletes outbox rows before confirms; a multi-service workflow treats retries as rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the inbox outbox composition increment in outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine consumer inbox deduplication, local domain effect, and outgoing outbox writes in one transaction to propagate effectively-once effects.
Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Inbox Outbox Composition · Theory Inbox Outbox Composition
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 2198 in a partner publisher using an unroutable changed topic key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; preserve one tenant fairness invariant; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Inbox Outbox Composition contract for the supplied data.
